items.q: Why does the left ventricle have a thicker muscular wall than the right?
items.correct: It must pump blood at high pressure around the whole body; the right ventricle only pumps to the nearby lungs.
items.misconception: The left ventricle is thicker because it handles oxygenated blood, which is harder to pump.
items.random_wrong: The left ventricle is thicker because it is on the same side as the brain.
items.why: Students focus on blood type (oxygenated vs not) rather than the pressure needed to push blood around the systemic circuit.

items.q: What is the role of the guide RNA in CRISPR-Cas9?
items.correct: It directs Cas9 to the specific DNA sequence to cut by base-pairing with the target.
items.misconception: Guide RNA is the new sequence that gets inserted into the genome.
items.random_wrong: Guide RNA is the enzyme that cuts the DNA.
items.why: Students confuse the guide RNA (which targets) with Cas9 (which cuts) and with any donor DNA (which is inserted) — three distinct components.

items.q: What is the key structural difference between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells?
items.correct: Eukaryotes have a membrane-bound nucleus and membrane-bound organelles; prokaryotes do not.
items.misconception: Prokaryotes have no DNA — that's why they have no nucleus.
items.random_wrong: Prokaryotes are always smaller than 1 micrometre and have no cytoplasm.
items.why: Students see 'no nucleus' and think 'no DNA', missing that prokaryotes still have DNA — it's just free in the cytoplasm.

items.q: Burnet's clonal selection theory of immunity dates from:
items.why: It's 1957. “1928” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Inserting aquaporins in collecting duct. “Closing aquaporins” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The real figure is 74 days. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
